args.report:
ATTORNEY REVIEW REQUIRED; NO LEGAL ADVICE

This draft amendment has been prepared by an AI assistant for discussion purposes only and does not constitute legal advice. It may not reflect all applicable laws or the parties’ actual agreement. The parties should consult their own licensed attorneys, who must review, revise, and approve this document before it is signed or relied upon.

---

FIRST AMENDMENT TO COMMERCIAL LEASE AGREEMENT

This FIRST AMENDMENT TO COMMERCIAL LEASE AGREEMENT (this "Amendment") is made as of [TBD] (the "Effective Date"), by and between [TBD], a [TBD] ("Landlord"), and [TBD], a [TBD] ("Tenant"). Landlord and Tenant are sometimes referred to individually as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties."

RECITALS

A. Landlord and Tenant are parties to that certain Commercial Lease Agreement dated as of [TBD] (the "Original Lease"), as [amended, supplemented, or modified by that certain [TBD] dated as of [TBD] (collectively with the Original Lease, the "Lease") / not previously amended (such Commercial Lease Agreement, the "Lease")].

B. Pursuant to the Lease, Tenant leases from Landlord certain premises consisting of approximately [TBD] rentable square feet, commonly known as [TBD], and more particularly described in the Lease (the "Premises").

C. Landlord and Tenant desire to amend the Lease solely to modify the monthly Base Rent payable under the Lease, on the terms and conditions set forth in this Amendment. Except as expressly modified by this Amendment, all terms and conditions of the Lease shall remain unchanged and in full force and effect.

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants and agreements contained in this Amendment and in the Lease, and for other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, Landlord and Tenant agree as follows:

1. DEFINITIONS; INTERPRETATION.

1.1 Defined Terms. Capitalized terms used but not defined in this Amendment shall have the meanings given to such terms in the Lease.

1.2 Lease References. All references in the Lease to "this Lease," "hereunder," "herein," "hereof," or words of similar import shall, from and after the Effective Date, be deemed to refer to the Lease as amended by this Amendment.

2. MODIFICATION OF BASE RENT.

2.1 Base Rent. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in the Lease, effective as of [TBD] (the "Rent Adjustment Date"), the monthly Base Rent payable by Tenant to Landlord for the Premises shall be [TBD] Dollars ($[TBD]) per month (the "New Base Rent"), payable in advance on the first day of each calendar month in accordance with the terms of the Lease.

2.2 Partial Month. If the Rent Adjustment Date is a day other than the first day of a calendar month, then the New Base Rent for the partial month in which the Rent Adjustment Date occurs shall be prorated on a per diem basis in accordance with the Lease.

2.3 No Other Rent Changes. Except as expressly set forth in Section 2.1 above, (a) all other monetary obligations of Tenant under the Lease, including without limitation additional rent, operating expenses, common area maintenance charges, taxes, insurance, utilities, percentage rent (if any), and any other charges or reimbursements, shall remain unchanged, and (b) the method and timing of payment of rent and other charges under the Lease shall remain as set forth in the Lease.

3. RATIFICATION; NO OTHER MODIFICATIONS.

3.1 Ratification of Lease. Except as expressly modified by this Amendment, the Lease is hereby ratified and confirmed in all respects and shall remain in full force and effect. The Parties acknowledge and agree that, as of the Effective Date, the Lease (as amended by this Amendment) is in full force and effect and constitutes the valid and binding obligation of the Parties, enforceable in accordance with its terms, subject to applicable bankruptcy, insolvency, and similar laws affecting creditors’ rights generally and general principles of equity.

3.2 No Other Amendments. This Amendment is limited to the matters expressly set forth herein. There are no oral or written understandings, agreements, or representations between the Parties with respect to the subject matter of this Amendment that are not expressly set forth in the Lease or this Amendment. In the event of any conflict or inconsistency between the terms of this Amendment and the terms of the Lease, the terms of this Amendment shall control with respect to the subject matter hereof, and the terms of the Lease shall control in all other respects.

3.3 No Waiver of Defaults. Nothing in this Amendment shall be deemed or construed as a waiver by Landlord of any existing or future default or breach by Tenant under the Lease, except to the limited extent (if any) expressly provided in a written waiver signed by Landlord.

4. REPRESENTATIONS; AUTHORITY.

4.1 Representations. Each Party represents and warrants to the other Party that (a) it has full power and authority to enter into this Amendment and to perform its obligations hereunder, (b) this Amendment has been duly authorized, executed, and delivered by such Party, and (c) this Amendment constitutes the legal, valid, and binding obligation of such Party, enforceable in accordance with its terms, subject to applicable bankruptcy, insolvency, and similar laws affecting creditors’ rights generally and general principles of equity.

4.2 Organization; Authorization. If Tenant is an entity, Tenant represents and warrants that it is duly organized, validly existing, and in good standing under the laws of its state of organization, and that the person(s) executing this Amendment on Tenant’s behalf are duly authorized to do so. If Landlord is an entity, Landlord makes a corresponding representation and warranty.

5. GOVERNING LAW; JURISDICTION AND VENUE.

5.1 Governing Law. This Amendment, and any dispute, claim, or controversy arising out of or relating to this Amendment or the Lease as amended hereby, shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of [TBD], without regard to its conflicts-of-law principles.

5.2 Jurisdiction and Venue. Subject to any mandatory arbitration or alternative dispute resolution provisions in the Lease, the Parties irrevocably submit to the exclusive [or: non-exclusive] jurisdiction of the state and federal courts located in [TBD County], [TBD] (the "Selected Courts") for any legal action or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Amendment or the Lease as amended hereby. Each Party hereby waives, to the fullest extent permitted by law, any objection that it may now or hereafter have to the laying of venue of any such action or proceeding in the Selected Courts, including any claim that any such action or proceeding has been brought in an inconvenient forum.

6. MISCELLANEOUS.

6.1 Counterparts; Electronic Signatures. This Amendment may be executed in one or more counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original and all of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument. Signatures delivered by facsimile, email (including .pdf), or other electronic means shall be deemed original signatures for all purposes.

6.2 Further Assurances. Each Party agrees to execute and deliver such additional documents and to take such further actions as may be reasonably necessary or desirable to carry out the intent and purposes of this Amendment.

6.3 No Third-Party Beneficiaries. This Amendment is for the sole benefit of Landlord and Tenant and their respective successors and permitted assigns, and nothing in this Amendment, express or implied, is intended to or shall confer upon any other person or entity any legal or equitable right, benefit, or remedy of any nature whatsoever.

6.4 Market-Standard and Jurisdiction-Specific Considerations. The Parties acknowledge that (a) this Amendment is intended to be consistent with market-standard commercial lease amendment practices for the jurisdiction in which the Premises are located, (b) certain jurisdiction-specific rules, including without limitation any requirements relating to notarization, recordation, transfer taxes, or registration of lease amendments, may apply depending on the location of the Premises and the governing law, and (c) the Parties’ respective counsel will confirm and implement any such requirements (including any flow-down obligations to subtenants, guarantors, or other third parties, and any updates to NNN/operating expense, maintenance, or similar matrices or exhibits) as necessary to ensure compliance with applicable law and the Parties’ business arrangements.

6.5 No Modification of NNN/Operating Expense or Maintenance Obligations. For the avoidance of doubt, this Amendment does not modify any provisions of the Lease relating to (a) triple-net (NNN) allocations, operating expenses, common area maintenance, or similar pass-throughs, or (b) maintenance, repair, or replacement obligations of Landlord or Tenant, except to the limited extent (if any) that such provisions are expressly tied to the amount of Base Rent and are automatically adjusted by operation of the Lease. Any required conforming changes to related exhibits, schedules, or matrices shall be prepared and implemented by the Parties’ counsel.

6.6 Flow-Down to Related Documents. To the extent there are any guaranties, subleases, estoppels, SNDA agreements, or other ancillary documents that reference the amount of Base Rent under the Lease, the Parties agree that their respective counsel will determine whether any amendments, consents, or confirmations are required to reflect the New Base Rent, and will prepare and obtain such documents as appropriate.

SIGNATURES

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have executed this Amendment as of the Effective Date.

Title: [TBD]
